confirmed.note: capture_thread_func() prologue (SetThreadPriority + wait_handles[] + while(is_running) + WaitForMultipleObjects + WAIT_OBJECT_0+1/WAIT_TIMEOUT/error dispatch) copied near-verbatim from render_thread_func():224-243, then body diverges to packet-drain; deinterleave loop also shared idiom

5 near-identical register_function("setX", [this](ArgumentList args){ id=get<string>(0); value=get<string>(1); v = id.empty()?&root_:widget(id); if(v) v->set_x(value); return Value(); }) blocks differing only in fn name + setter (setMaskSize/setAppearance/setObjectFit/setObjectPosition); JS-binding registration boilerplate

which() PATH-scan helper (getenv(PATH) + path_str.find(':',start) split loop + fs::path(dir)/name candidate) re-implemented to avoid cli_common dependency; same loop as default_find_executable() in tools/cli/mac_runtime_validators.cpp:55-80 with renamed vars. Comment itself admits 'minimal lookup locally'

false_positives.what_happened: Catch2 test bodies share fixed setup-then-assert skeleton: 'View root; InspectorOverlay overlay(root); KeyEvent ke; ke.key=...; ke.is_down=true; REQUIRE(overlay.handle_key_event(ke)); REQUIRE(overlay.is_active());'. Only the KeyCode and the asserted predicate change across toggle/Escape/inactive/Z-loupe cases. The token-level n-gram overlap is high because the harness scaffolding repeats, but each case tests a distinct behavior.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: down-weight matches whose differing tokens are concentrated in literal/enum positions while the control-flow + call skeleton is identical AND the block lives in a *_test/test_*.cpp TU — add a test-fixture suppressor: if >70% of shared tokens are calls to a known assertion macro family (REQUIRE/CHECK/EXPECT/ASSERT/SECTION) require a higher line-similarity floor (e.g. line>=0.95) before reporting. Already-present test-file weighting should be tightened, not just flagged.

false_positives.what_happened: Cluster of one-line forwarding accessors 'std::optional<float> NativeBindingMetadata::thumb_width_value() const { return parse_float_opt(thumb_width); }' repeated per field (thumb_width/thumb_height/thumb_corner_radius/corner_radius/font_size...). Each is a trivial 1-statement getter forwarding to a shared helper — the textbook accessor idiom.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: enforce a minimum non-boilerplate-statement count (>=3 meaningful statements, excluding the single return) per candidate block, and a min-distinct-token threshold; single-return forwarder bodies should never reach the matcher. archcheck duplication layer should treat a function whose body is exactly one return-of-call as exempt.

false_positives.what_happened: Pure split refactor: 'refactor(view): split design_import.cpp JSON ser/de into design_ir_json.cpp (#3141)' MOVED serializer/parser blocks out of design_import.cpp into the new design_ir_json.cpp. The detector (at the later changelog-bot snapshot) reported verbatim cross-file dup between the two files, but the parse_* functions are now partitioned (each lives in only one TU) — it is a code MOVE, not residual duplication. The 'BASE' side reflects pre-split history / shared header-driven idioms, not a live second copy.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: rename/move-aware diffing. When a new file's added block matches a block DELETED from another file in the same commit (or the BASE block no longer exists at HEAD), classify as MOVE and suppress. Concretely: before emitting a cross-file pair, verify both source ranges still exist at HEAD; if the BASE range was removed/relocated, drop the finding (same git-rename heuristic already used for whole-file moves, extended to intra-commit block relocation).
